reason: The remediation modifies the GRUB2 bootloader configuration to add a password, which is a bootloader change. According to policy, bootloader modifications are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically due to the high risk of rendering the system unbootable or locking out console access.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; Potential bootloader lockout; Loss of console or physical access if password is misconfigured; System may fail to boot until password is corrected
